content.question: What is the causal contribution of early academic selection via 6/8-year programs to ethnic and Roma school segregation, versus residential location, parental choice constraints, and the effects of later (age 14) secondary tracking?
content.reduction_path: Analyze administrative enrollment data disaggregated by ethnicity/Roma status, tracking entrance exam participation and success rates by school, home address, and family characteristics; compare segregation levels and trends in regions with varying concentrations of 6/8-year programs while controlling for residential distribution and vocational-track uptake.
